data.title: U.S. steelmakers urge Trump to resist tariff exclusions
data.description:
The CEOs of the three largest U.S. steel producers urged President Trump on Friday to resist requests to exempt steel imports from upcoming 25% tariffs.

Nine steel industry executives including Nucor's (NYSE:NUE) Leon Topalian, US Steel's (X) David Burritt and Cleveland-Cliffs' (CLF) Lourenco Goncalves sent a letter to Trump saying previous exemptions given to nations allowed for increased import volumes that lowered the original impact of the president's levies during his first administration.

"Millions of tons of product-specific exclusions were granted, even for products readily available from domestic suppliers. The result was a weakened U.S. steel industry exposed again to the global steel oversupply crisis," the letter said, according to Bloomberg. "We urge you to resist any requests for exceptions or exclusions and to continue standing strong on behalf of American steel."

The looming threat of tariffs has raised prices for American steel in recent weeks, making it more than 20% more expensive than imported steel; steel was selling for less than $700/ton as recently as January, but by the end of February, domestic producers were said to be quoting customers prices as high as $1,000/ton, a level not seen since the beginning of last year.

High-ranking executives of Commercial Metals (CMC), Metallus (MTUS), North American Stainless, Steel Dynamics (STLD), Tenaris (TS) and Zekelman Industries also signed the letter.

data.title: DoorDash, TKO, Williams-Sonoma, Expand Energy joining S&P 500
data.description:
DoorDash (DASH), WWE’s parent TKO (NYSE:TKO), and two other companies will be added to the S&P 500 (SP500) this month, and four names will drop in exchange. 

High-end housewares retailer Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) and natural gas producer Expand Energy (NASDAQ:EXE) will also enter the benchmark index (SP500), S&P Dow Jones Indices said late Friday. The changes are effective before trading opens March 24. 

At the same time, Borgwarner (NYSE:BWA), Teleflex (NYSE:TFX) and Celanese (NYSE:CE) will exit along with agricultural chemicals company FMC (NYSE:FMC). 

“All companies being added to the S&P 500 are more representative of the large-cap market space,” the index provider said. It’s also making changes in the S&P 100 (SP100), S&P MidCap 400, and the S&P SmallCap 600 indexes. 

S&P 500 (SP500) ETFs include: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

(NYSEARCA:MDY) is among mid-cap ETFs such as: (IJH), (VO), and (SCHM). 

And here are some small-cap ETFs: (NYSEARCA:IJR), (IWM), (VB), (VBR), (VBK), (SCHA), (SPSM), and (DFAS).

data.title: Gold gains for 9th week in 10 on safe-haven demand; China continues gold purchases
data.description:
Gold futures fell modestly on Friday but gained for the week due to safe-haven inflows driven by geopolitical uncertainty, tariff tensions and weak economic growth.

Gold edged lower Friday following the release of the Labor Department report that said the U.S. economy added 151K jobs in February, slightly lower than expected, while the unemployment rate ticked higher to 4.1%, sending the U.S. dollar index tumbling to a four-month low and tallying its steepest weekly decline since November 2022, making greenback-priced bullion less expensive for foreign buyers.

The gold market is currently in a consolidation phase, with safe haven interest providing ongoing support, Zaner Metals senior metals strategist Peter Grant said.

Meanwhile, China continued its gold purchases for the fourth consecutive month in February, with its reserves rising to 73.61M troy oz valued at $208.64B from 73.45M valued at $206.53B at the end of January, according to data from the People's Bank of China.

"The PBOC's purchases are an important factor underpinning gold, so a continuation of its buying in February could help to build further strength behind the gold price," Kinese Money analyst Frank Watson said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery ended +2.4% at $2,904.70/oz this week, and front-month March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) closed +4.2% to $32.548/oz this week; on Friday, gold fell 0.4% and silver slid 1.5%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Newmont (NEM) expanded its position as the world's top gold producing company last year, amassing 6.7M oz, according to Bloomberg research, thanks to added output from its 2023 takeover of Australia's Newcrest Mining.

Barrick Gold (GOLD) was the runner-up with 3.9M oz produced in 2024, but its lead over Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) narrowed after output fell due to operational setbacks; Agnico Eagle threatens to overtake its bigger rival if Barrick's mine complex in Mali remains closed this year.

The next five names on the list of last year's top producers: Russia's Polyus, AngloGold Ashanti (AU), Zijin Mining (OTCPK:ZIJMF), Gold Fields (GFI) and Kinross Gold (KGC).

All NYSE-traded names show strong YTD gains: Newmont (NEM) +17.9%, Barrick (GOLD) +20%, Agnico Eagle (AEM) +26.3%, AngloGold (AU) +36.4%, Gold Fields (GFI) +52.9%, Kinross (KGC) +20.3%.

data.title: Hims & Hers abandons dermatology business Apostrophe it paid $190M for
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) shut down Apostrophe on Friday, the skincare business it acquired in 2019 for $190M.

A message at the top of Apostrophe's website says, "Apostrophe is being discontinued and all current subscriptions will be canceled as of March 7th, 2025," adding that the platform will go dark in 30 days.

Hims & Hers told Business Insider that the decision was made "to simplify our dermatology products and operations into one seamless experience for our customers."  

The company offers a minoxidil tablet for hair loss, as well as various skin care creams for both men and women.

The shuttering of Apostrophe comes shortly after a March 5 decision by a federal judge to deny an injunction request from a compounding medication trade group to allow it to continue making versions of Eli Lilly's (LLY) tirzepatide, the active ingredient in the weight loss drug Zepbound and type 2 diabetes med Mounjaro.

Hims & Hers markets a compounded version of Novo Nordisk's (NVO) semaglutide, which is found in Wegovy (weight loss) and Ozempic (diabetes). The same organization, the Outsourcing Facilities Association, has also filed suit aiming to protect sales of compounded semaglutide. 

Compounded versions of drugs under patent protection are permitted if that drug is in shortage. However, the U.S. FDA has determined that all four brand-name drugs are no longer in shortage.

data.title: Vaccinex plans to delist common stock from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Vaccinex (OTC:VCNX) on Friday announced that it has notified the Nasdaq of its decision to delist the company’s shares of common stock from Nasdaq.
  • Trading in the common stock on Nasdaq has been suspended since December 18, 2024.
  • Vaccinex intends to file a Form 25 with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or about March 17, 2025 to remove its common stock from listing on Nasdaq.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: EpicQuest Education falls on receipt of Nasdaq delinquency notification letter
data.description:
• EpicQuest Education Group (NASDAQ:EEIQ) said on Friday that it received a delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq on March 5, 2025.
  • The letter indicated that the company is not currently in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement set forth in Nasdaq's Listing Rules as the closing bid price for the company's common shares was below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days.
  • The letter provides that the company has a period of 180 calendar days from the date of the letter, or until September 1, 2025, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • EEIQ -4.23% after hours to $0.7719.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Valaris files to sell 4.76M common shares by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Valaris (NYSE:VAL) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 15 million shares of common stocks.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing
